weeks.label: Week 1 — Ball Control & Dribbling
weeks.location: Sanborn Elementary School Lower Field, Andover, MA

prek.title: Red Light, Green Light
prek.desc: Every player gets a ball. "Green light" — dribble forward. "Red light" — freeze with a foot on top of the ball. Listening, control, confidence right from the start.
focus: Soft touches with the inside of the foot
Stopping the ball — foot on top

prek.title: Friendly Foot — Cone Path
prek.desc: Simple cone path. Each player takes turns dribbling slowly through with small touches. Coach cheers every touch. Get comfortable with the ball at your feet.
focus: Keep the ball close — small touches
Use both feet, even just a little

prek.title: Toe Taps & Bell Touches
prek.desc: Standing still, players tap the top of the ball with alternating feet. Then tap the ball side-to-side between feet ("bells"). Builds feel for the ball.

prek.title: Everyone Scores Mini Game
prek.desc: Tiny-sided game on a small field. No goalies. Coach makes sure every kid gets at least one shot on goal. Cheering, celebrating, lots of giggles.
focus: Run toward the ball, not away from it
Take a swing — shooting is fun

elem.title: Sharks & Minnows
elem.desc: Everyone gets a ball except 1–2 sharks. Sharks try to kick everyone else's ball out. Last minnow standing wins. Dribbling under pressure from the first whistle.

elem.title: Close-Control Dribbling
elem.desc: Slalom through cones using inside, outside, and sole of the foot. Three speeds: slow technique → game speed → competition (timed). Build close control under pressure.
focus: Acceleration out of the last cone

elem.title: 1v1 to the Line
elem.desc: Two players, one ball. Attacker tries to dribble across the defender's endline. Defender tries to win it. Rotate after each rep. Real 1v1 reps build confidence.
focus: Change of direction to beat pressure

elem.title: Small-Sided Scrimmage
elem.desc: 4v4 or 5v5 with real goals and goalies. Apply everything from the session in live play. Coach freezes the action a few times to celebrate good decisions.
focus: Take players on with the dribble
Read the game — when to dribble, when to pass

weeks.label: Week 2 — Passing & First Touch
weeks.location: Sanborn Elementary School Lower Field, Andover, MA

prek.title: Pass the Coach
prek.desc: Players stand in a circle around the coach. Roll the ball, then later pass it to the coach. Coach passes it back. Lots of high-fives, lots of reps.
focus: Inside of the foot — the 'passing foot'
Watch the ball into your foot

prek.title: Stop & Pass
prek.desc: Partner rolls the ball. Player stops it with the sole, then passes it back. Strict 2-step rhythm. Build the basic first-touch-then-pass habit.
focus: Stop with the bottom of the foot

prek.title: Pass Through the Gate
prek.desc: Partners face each other with a 2-cone gate in the middle. Pass the ball through the gate. Move further apart as they succeed.
focus: Right pace — not too hard, not too soft

prek.title: Everyone Scores Mini Game
prek.desc: Small-sided game. Coach reminds players to pass to a teammate when they can. Every kid gets a shot. Goals celebrated, no scorekeeping.

elem.title: Pass Tag
elem.desc: Two taggers with balls — they tag others by passing the ball into them. Everyone else dribbles to avoid. Switch taggers every minute.

elem.title: 2-Touch Partner Passing
elem.desc: Strict 2-touch: first touch settles, second touch passes back. Add movement — pass and follow your pass. Builds clean technique and rhythm.
focus: Communicate — eye contact, call for the ball

elem.title: Pass & Move Triangles
elem.desc: Three players, three cones. Pass to the next cone, then sprint to the empty cone. Continuous movement. Mirrors real positional play.
focus: Pass and move — never stand still

elem.title: Small-Sided Scrimmage
elem.desc: 4v4 or 5v5. Coach rule: every goal must follow at least one pass. Forces players to look up and link with teammates.

weeks.label: Week 3 — Driving with the Ball
weeks.location: Sanborn Elementary School Lower Field, Andover, MA

prek.title: Follow the Coach
prek.desc: Coach jogs around with their ball. Players follow, copying everything — change of direction, sudden stops, speed changes. A moving warm-up they love.

prek.title: Push & Chase
prek.desc: Players push the ball ahead with the laces, then sprint to catch up and stop it. Builds the feeling of driving forward at speed.
focus: Push with the laces — not the toe
Stop it cleanly at the end

prek.title: Race to the Cone
prek.desc: Two players race, dribbling their ball to the far cone and back. Light competition, lots of laughs. Build the urge to go fast with the ball.
focus: Stop the ball at the end

prek.title: Everyone Scores Mini Game
prek.desc: Small-sided fun game. Coach encourages players to "run with the ball" toward the goal. Everyone gets a shot.

elem.title: Sharks & Minnows
elem.desc: Everyone gets a ball, 1–2 sharks try to kick them out. Forces dribbling at speed under pressure. Last minnow wins.

elem.title: First Touch & Drive
elem.desc: Partner passes the ball. Player takes a directional first touch into space and drives forward 5 yards. Pass back. Switch roles. The point: first touch goes where you want to go.
focus: Open your body before the ball arrives
Drive at pace after the touch

elem.title: Drive & Finish
elem.desc: Receive a pass, take one driving touch toward goal, finish. Both feet, both sides. After the first round, add a defender chasing — finish under pressure.
focus: Plant foot, lock ankle, follow through
Decide quickly — shoot or another touch

elem.title: Small-Sided Scrimmage
elem.desc: 4v4 or 5v5. Coach challenge: at least one driving run with the ball per goal scored. Rewards bravery on the ball.
focus: Carry the ball when space is open

weeks.label: Week 4 — Shooting & Finishing
weeks.location: Sanborn Elementary School Lower Field, Andover, MA

prek.title: Knock Down the Cones
prek.desc: Coach sets up a line of stand-up cones. Players take turns kicking their ball to knock them over. Fun, competitive, lots of swings at the ball.
focus: Cheer for every cone knocked down

prek.title: Shoot the Ball
prek.desc: Lined up 10 yards from a small goal. One at a time, players take a shot. Coach corrects technique gently — plant foot beside the ball, swing through.

prek.title: Score 5 Goals
prek.desc: In pairs, players race to score 5 goals each. Lots of reps in a short time. The goal is to make shooting feel normal and fun.

prek.title: Everyone Scores Mini Game
prek.desc: Small-sided game. Coach makes sure every kid gets at least two shots. Goals celebrated with all the high-fives.
focus: Shoot when you see the goal

elem.title: Hot Potato Shooting
elem.desc: Players in a circle pass to each other. When the coach yells "shoot!" whoever has the ball drives in and finishes. Fast decisions under pressure.
focus: Be ready to receive at any moment

elem.title: Finishing from a Pass
elem.desc: Coach plays a pass. Player runs onto it and finishes first-time or with one touch. Both feet. Far post, near post, low driven — call the shot.
focus: Run onto the ball, don't wait
Pick a corner before you arrive

elem.title: 2v1 to Goal
elem.desc: Two attackers vs one defender, attacking a real goal. Combine to create a clean shot. Real decision-making: pass for the finish, or drive yourself?

elem.title: Small-Sided Scrimmage
elem.desc: 4v4 or 5v5. Coach calls out great finishes and brave shots. Encourage players to shoot whenever they see a chance.

weeks.label: Week 5 — 1v1 Attacking
weeks.location: Sanborn Elementary School Lower Field, Andover, MA

prek.title: Red Light, Green Light
prek.desc: Returning favorite. Players dribble on green, freeze on red. This week we add "yellow light" — slow dribbling — for a third challenge.

prek.title: Go Around the Coach
prek.desc: Coach stands still as a friendly "defender". Players take turns dribbling around the coach to the other side. Build the idea of taking someone on.
focus: Don't kick it past — keep it close
Go fast after you pass the coach

prek.title: Beat the Cone
prek.desc: Players dribble through a short cone slalom, pretending each cone is a defender. Coach cheers cuts and turns.
focus: Touch on both sides of the cone

prek.title: Everyone Scores Mini Game
prek.desc: Small-sided. Coach gently rewards players who try to dribble past someone — even if it doesn't work out.
focus: Don't give up if you lose it

elem.title: 1v1 King of the Hill
elem.desc: Quick 1v1s in small boxes. Winner stays, loser rotates out. Fast, competitive, high-rep. Sets the tone for the day.

elem.title: Two Moves to Beat a Defender
elem.desc: Teach the scissor and the inside-outside cut. Slow walk-through, then full speed. Players use the moves against a passive then active defender.
focus: Explosive touch on the second move

elem.title: 1v1 to Goal
elem.desc: One attacker, one defender, attacking the goal. Real 1v1 with finishing. Both players compete hard. Switch roles each rep.

elem.title: Small-Sided Scrimmage
elem.desc: 4v4 or 5v5. Coach rule: bonus point for any goal scored after beating a defender 1v1. Encourages bravery on the ball.
focus: Don't be afraid to lose it

weeks.label: Week 6 — Combination Play
weeks.location: Sanborn Elementary School Lower Field, Andover, MA

prek.title: Pass the Coach
prek.desc: Players in a circle, coach in the middle. Pass to coach, coach passes back. Coach calls out names — "good job Sammy!" Tons of touches.
focus: Watch the ball into your foot

prek.title: Pass & Run
prek.desc: Partner A passes to partner B, then immediately runs to a new spot. Partner B passes it back to the new spot. Build the habit of moving after passing.
focus: Pass and go — don't stand still
Find your partner with your eyes

prek.title: Pass Through the Gate
prek.desc: Multiple cone gates scattered. Pairs pass through as many as they can in a minute. Counting goals together = teamwork.
focus: Move to a new gate together

prek.title: Everyone Scores Mini Game
prek.desc: Small-sided. Coach calls out great passes — "that was a great pass!" Reinforces the habit gently in live play.

elem.title: 3-Team Possession
elem.desc: Three teams of 3. Two teams combine to keep the ball from the third. Loser rotates in. Fast, fun, lots of passing reps.

elem.title: Give-and-Go (Wall Pass)
elem.desc: Player A passes to player B, runs past defender, B plays it back into A's path. The classic 1-2. Walk through, then add a defender.

elem.title: Combination to Goal
elem.desc: 2v1 attacking a real goal. Use the give-and-go to break the defender and finish. Real game-speed reps.
focus: Use the wall pass to break pressure

elem.title: Small-Sided Scrimmage
elem.desc: 4v4 or 5v5. Coach challenge: at least one give-and-go combination per goal scored. Builds it into game habit.

weeks.label: Week 7 — Game Application & Celebration
weeks.location: Sanborn Elementary School Lower Field, Andover, MA

prek.title: Coach's Choice Warm-Up
prek.desc: Kids vote between Red Light Green Light, Follow the Coach, or Pass the Coach. Their favorite from the season. Big energy, big smiles.

prek.title: Skills Showcase
prek.desc: Kids show what they've learned: toe taps, stops, passes. Coach calls out names and gives every kid a "superstar move" to perform. Confidence builder.

prek.title: Pass & Shoot Combo
prek.desc: Pass to coach, coach passes back, player shoots at the goal. Combines passing and shooting from the season. Lots of celebrations.
focus: Take the shot when it comes

prek.title: Everyone Scores Celebration Game
prek.desc: Last small-sided game of the season. Every kid scores at least twice. Coach celebrates each goal like the World Cup final.

elem.title: World Cup Knockout
elem.desc: Pairs play 1v1. Winner stays, loser is out. Tournament-style bracket. Players' favorite competitive game.

elem.title: Free Play with a Theme
elem.desc: Coach picks a theme from the season — passing, driving, finishing, 1v1, combinations — and players run through it one last time at full speed. Mastery rep.

elem.title: Position-Based Play
elem.desc: Quick chalk talk on positions — striker, midfielder, defender, goalie. Walk through where each goes. Players try a new position they haven't played.
focus: Where do I go without the ball?

elem.title: Season Finale Scrimmage
elem.space: Full available field, two real goals
elem.desc: Final scrimmage. Real positions, real goals. Coach celebrates great moments throughout — passes, runs, defending, finishes. End-of-season vibes.

prek.title: Red Light, Green Light — Big Field Edition
prek.desc: Every player gets a ball on our brand-new full field. "Green light" — dribble forward. "Red light" — freeze with a foot on top of the ball. "Yellow light" — slow-motion dribbling. Listening, control, and confidence to kick off the summer.
focus: Soft touches with the inside of the foot
Stop the ball — foot on top

prek.title: Toe Taps, Bells & Sole Rolls
prek.desc: Ball-mastery circuit: toe taps on top of the ball, side-to-side "bells" between the feet, then rolling the ball with the sole. 30 seconds per move, coach counts touches out loud. Building the feel that everything else grows from.
focus: Light, quick touches — stay on your toes

prek.title: Catch the Rolling Ball
prek.desc: Partner rolls the ball. Player cushions it softly with the inside of the foot — like catching an egg — then dribbles two touches and passes it back. First touch made simple and fun.
focus: First touch out in front, not stuck under you
Watch the ball into your foot

prek.title: 5v5 on Proper Goals
prek.desc: Our first big-field scrimmage of the summer — 5v5 with real full-size goals for the first time! Coach makes sure every kid gets a shot on the big net. Goals celebrated loudly.
focus: Keep your first touch close in the game
Shoot at the big goal — it's huge!

elem.title: Ball Mastery Gauntlet
elem.desc: Everyone dribbling in the grid. Coach calls surfaces — inside only, outside only, sole rolls, pull-back turns — while players avoid traffic. Last call: game speed. Sets a summer standard of comfort on the ball.
focus: All four surfaces of the foot
Head up while dribbling in traffic
Sharp change of speed on command

elem.title: First Touch Out of the Air & Off the Ground
elem.desc: Partner serves firm ground passes, then light tosses. Receiver kills the ball with one touch — inside of foot for ground balls, thigh or laces cushion for tosses — and plays back in two touches. Full field means real space to punish a bad touch, so we make the first touch elite.
focus: Cushion — pull the surface back on contact
First touch away from imaginary pressure
Get in line with the flight of the ball

elem.title: Receive–Turn–Drive
elem.desc: Server passes in, receiver checks to the ball, opens the body on the first touch, turns, and drives 10 yards to the end of the channel. Add a shadow defender behind the receiver to force a real scan before receiving.
focus: Scan over your shoulder before the ball arrives
Open your hips — receive across your body
Explode into space after the turn

elem.title: 7v7 Big-Field Scrimmage
elem.desc: First large-format scrimmage of the season — 7v7 (or 6v6) on the full outdoor field with proper goals and goalkeepers. Coach rule: clean first touch before every pass or shot. Feel the bigger space and longer passes.
focus: First touch sets up your next action
Use the width of the big field

prek.title: Pass the Coach
prek.desc: Players in a circle around the coach, each with a ball. Pass to the coach, coach passes it back with your name and a high-five. Tons of reps of the inside-of-the-foot pass right away.
focus: Inside of the foot — the 'passing foot'
Watch the ball into your foot

prek.title: Stop & Pass
prek.desc: Partner passes, player stops the ball with the sole, then passes back with the inside of the foot. Strict two-step rhythm: stop... pass. As pairs improve, take away the stop — one-touch challenge!
focus: Stop with the bottom of the foot
Push through the middle of the ball

prek.title: Gate Passing Adventure
prek.desc: Cone gates scattered across our big field. Pairs pass through as many different gates as they can in 90 seconds, then try to beat their score. Passing plus moving together — sneaky teamwork practice.
focus: Aim — pick your gate before you pass
Right pace — partner shouldn't chase it
Move to a new gate together

prek.title: 5v5 on Proper Goals
prek.desc: 5v5 on the big goals. Coach rule this week: try one pass before shooting. Every pass that leads to a shot gets a loud "great pass!" from the coach.
focus: Pass to a teammate when you can
Run to support after you pass

elem.title: 4-Corner Passing Race
elem.desc: One player per cone plus one chaser in the middle. Pass around the square while the chaser hunts the ball. Two-touch max. Switch the chaser on every giveaway. Pressure passing from minute one.
focus: Pass away from the chaser's run
Crisp weight — passes that beat pressure
Receive on the back foot, ready to play

elem.title: Pass & Move Triangles
elem.desc: Pass to the next cone, sprint to the empty cone. Then flip direction on the coach's call so players receive on the other foot. Finish with a two-touch time trial — most completed passes in 60 seconds wins.
focus: Pass and move — never admire your pass
Open body shape to see two cones at once

elem.title: 3v1 Rondo to Break the Line
elem.desc: Three attackers keep the ball from one defender. A "split pass" through the defender counts double. Defender out after two wins or 45 seconds. Real decisions: when to play safe around, when to play brave through.
focus: Angle of support — give the passer two options
First touch away from the defender

elem.title: 7v7 Big-Field Scrimmage
elem.desc: 7v7 on the full field with proper goals. Coach rule: every goal must follow at least two completed passes. The bigger field rewards switching play — coach highlights every long diagonal that finds space.
focus: Look for the pass before the dribble
Switch the field when one side is crowded

prek.title: Sharks & Minnows
prek.desc: Everyone gets a ball except 1–2 sharks. Sharks try to kick the minnows' balls out of the grid. Last minnow standing wins and becomes a shark. Dribbling under (friendly) pressure from the first whistle.
focus: Keep the ball close when a shark is near
Head up — see the sharks coming

prek.title: Superhero Moves — The Pull-Back & The Cut
prek.desc: Two "superhero moves": the pull-back (sole drags the ball back, spin away) and the inside cut (chop the ball across your body). Walk-through, then slow-motion, then fast. Coach gives each move a superhero name the kids pick.
focus: Sole on top for the pull-back
Big chop across the body for the cut

prek.title: Beat the Coach
prek.desc: Coach (or a helper) is a friendly, slow-motion defender. Players take turns using a superhero move to get past and dribble to the far line. Every successful move gets a celebration. Builds the courage to take someone on.
focus: Try your move — don't just kick and run
Keep the ball close through the move

prek.title: 5v5 on Proper Goals
prek.desc: 5v5 big-field game. Coach cheers loudest for anyone brave enough to try a superhero move — even when it doesn't work. Bravery on the ball is the win this week.
focus: Don't give up if you lose it — chase back

elem.title: 1v1 King of the Hill
elem.desc: Rapid-fire 1v1s — dribble across your opponent's line to win. Winner stays on, loser rotates. 30-second rounds, no breaks. High-rep dueling to set the tone.

elem.title: Three Moves to Beat a Defender
elem.space: 12 yd lanes with a mannequin cone
elem.desc: Scissor, stepover, and the inside-outside cut. Slow walk-through for mechanics, then full speed at a cone "defender", then against a passive partner. Each player picks a go-to move by the end.
focus: Sell the fake with your whole body
Big explosive touch out of the move
Change of pace is the real move

elem.title: 1v1 to Twin Gates
elem.space: 15x12 yd boxes, 2 gate goals per box
elem.desc: Attacker faces a live defender and can score through EITHER of two cone gates on the endline. Forces a real decision: beat the defender to the left gate or the right? Rotate roles every rep, count wins.
focus: Read the defender's hips, attack the open side
Use your go-to move at speed
Shield and re-attack if the first move fails

elem.title: 7v7 Big-Field Scrimmage
elem.desc: 7v7 with proper goals. Bonus point for any goal that comes after a successful 1v1 take-on. The full field gives real space to run at defenders — coach demands bravery in the attacking half.
focus: Take defenders on in the attacking third
Dribble into space, pass under pressure
Recover hard when you lose it

prek.title: Knock Down the Cones
prek.desc: A line of stand-up cones between two teams. Players blast their balls to knock down more cones than the other team. Fun, loud, and a hundred shooting reps before anyone realizes they're practicing.
focus: Look at your target before you kick

prek.title: Shoot on the BIG Goal
prek.desc: This is the week we've been waiting for — real full-size goals! Players line up 10 yards out and take shots one at a time. Coach fixes technique gently: plant foot beside the ball, laces through the middle. The big net makes every kid feel like a pro.
focus: Plant foot points at the goal
Strike with the laces, ankle locked
Watch the ball, not the goal, at contact

prek.title: Dribble & Shoot Race
prek.space: Two 15 yd lanes to the proper goal
prek.desc: Two lines race: dribble through two cones, then shoot on the big goal. First team to 10 goals wins. Combines dribbling from last week with this week's shooting — lots of reps, lots of noise.
focus: Last touch sets up the shot
Celebrate every goal like the World Cup

prek.title: 5v5 on Proper Goals
prek.desc: 5v5 where every kid is encouraged to shoot the moment they see the big goal. Coach guarantees at least two shots per player. Missed shots get a "great try, shoot again!"
focus: Shoot when you see the goal
Follow your shot for the rebound

elem.title: Hot Potato Shooting
elem.space: 18 yd arc to a proper goal
elem.desc: Players pass around the arc. When the coach yells "SHOOT!", whoever has the ball takes a touch inside and finishes on the full-size goal with a keeper. Instant decisions, real goal, real keeper.
focus: Always ready — the call can come anytime

elem.title: Finishing Clinic on Proper Goals
elem.space: 20 yd channel to goal, with keeper
elem.desc: Three finishes on the full-size goal: low far-post placement, driven laces strike, and first-time finish off a coach's pass. Both feet. Full-size goals reward corners — the keeper can't reach everything anymore, so we aim for the frame's edges.
focus: Placement beats power — low and to the corner
Lock the ankle on the driven strike
Pick your finish before the ball arrives

elem.title: 2v1 + Keeper to Goal
elem.desc: Two attackers vs one defender plus a goalkeeper on the big goal. Combine to create a clean strike. The real decision: draw the defender and slip your partner in, or shoot yourself? Defender wins by clearing past halfway.
focus: Defender commits → pass; defender waits → shoot
Finish across the keeper to the far post
One-touch finishes when the pass is good

elem.title: 7v7 Big-Field Scrimmage
elem.desc: 7v7 with keepers on both proper goals. Coach challenge: shots from outside the "box" cones count double. The full field plus real goals means real shooting angles — let it fly.
focus: Shoot on sight in the attacking third

prek.title: Body Part Dribble
prek.desc: Everyone dribbles in the grid. Coach calls a body part — "knee!" "elbow!" "nose!" — and players stop the ball and touch it with that body part as fast as they can. Giggles guaranteed, coordination hidden inside the fun.
focus: Quick stop — foot on the ball

prek.title: Obstacle Course Adventure
prek.desc: A winding course on our big field: slalom through cones with the ball, hop through hoops (leave the ball, hop, come back), dribble the ladder lines, finish with a sprint. Each lap timed loosely — race your own time, not each other.
focus: Quick feet through the tight parts
Balance — land softly on the hops
Ball close in the slalom, big touch in the sprint

prek.title: Speedy Turns Relay
prek.desc: Relay race: dribble to the far cone, do a pull-back turn (superhero move from Week 3!), dribble back, tag the next runner. Turning at speed is the secret skill hiding in the race.
focus: Slow down INTO the turn, speed up OUT
Use the sole for the pull-back

prek.title: 5v5 on Proper Goals
prek.desc: 5v5 big-field game. Coach calls out every great burst of speed and sharp turn — "look at those quick feet!" All that agility work shows up in the chasing and turning of the real game.
focus: Turn fast when the game turns

elem.title: Mirror 1v1 (No Tackling)
elem.desc: Attacker dribbles side-to-side on their line; defender mirrors without a ball. On the coach's whistle the attacker breaks for the end line, defender races to tag. Reaction speed, lateral quickness, and change of pace with the ball.
focus: Low athletic stance — ready to cut
Sharp lateral touches to shift the defender
Explode on the change of pace

elem.title: Speed & Turn Circuit with the Ball
elem.desc: Four stations, 90 seconds each: (1) slalom sprint dribble, (2) 5–10–5 shuttle with the ball, (3) turn box — receive, Cruyff or pull-back, exit a different gate, (4) 20 yd flat-out sprint dribble with only 3 touches allowed. Full field means we can finally train real running speed WITH the ball.
focus: Big touches into space, small touches in traffic
Decelerate before the turn, accelerate out
Pump the arms — sprint mechanics still count with a ball

elem.title: Race to Finish
elem.space: 25 yd from halfway to a proper goal
elem.desc: Coach plays a through ball; two players sprint from staggered starts — attacker chasing to finish on the big goal, defender chasing to clear. First to the ball decides everything. Pure game-speed: sprint, control at pace, finish under a chasing body.
focus: Control at full sprint — soft touch, don't break stride
Composed finish with a defender on your shoulder

elem.title: 7v7 Big-Field Scrimmage
elem.desc: 7v7 with proper goals. Coach rule: any breakaway won by a sprint (attacking OR recovering) gets a shout-out. The big field is a speed field — this week's work shows up in every long run.
focus: Sprint into space behind the defense
Use the whole field to stretch them

weeks.label: Week 6 — Juggling, Aerial Control & Combination Play

prek.title: Balloon Ball / Catch-Juggle
prek.desc: Players drop the ball from their hands, let it bounce once, kick it back up, and catch it. Count catches out loud together. The very first step of juggling — made easy enough that everyone succeeds.
focus: Kick the middle of the ball, gently

prek.title: Bounce Juggling Challenge
prek.desc: Drop, bounce, kick, bounce, kick — how many can you do? Coach tracks each player's personal record and celebrates every new best. Thigh touches allowed for a bonus point. Pure feel-for-the-ball practice.
focus: Soft touch — the ball should float, not fly
Beat YOUR record, not your neighbor's

prek.title: Pass, Follow & Shoot
prek.desc: Three-player combo: pass to the coach, run forward, get the ball back, pass to a teammate, and the teammate shoots on the big goal. Then rotate. First taste of link-up play, finished with a bang.
focus: Finish the team move with a shot

prek.title: 5v5 on Proper Goals
prek.desc: 5v5. Coach rule: a goal after a pass-and-follow combo counts double. Kids start hunting for teammates — the beginning of real team soccer.
focus: One pass, then look for the return

elem.title: Juggling Ladder
elem.desc: Everyone juggles: feet only, then feet + thigh, then the sequence right-left-thigh. One bounce allowed between touches for those who need it. Coach records each player's summer PR — we'll beat it next week.
focus: Small touches at waist height max
Reset with a bounce, don't chase a wild ball

elem.title: Aerial First Touch
elem.desc: Partner tosses: (1) laces cushion out of the air, (2) thigh kill, (3) chest-drop to feet. Two touches to control and pass back on the ground. Full field means goal kicks and long balls are real now — aerial control is a weapon.
focus: Move your feet early — get under the flight
Cushion on contact — surface moves WITH the ball
First touch down to the grass, ready to play

elem.title: Give-and-Go to Goal
elem.space: 30x20 yd zone to a proper goal, with keeper
elem.desc: Attacker plays a wall pass with a target player to break past a live defender, then finishes on the big goal. Progress to 2v2 + target: combos must beat real pressure. The classic 1-2 at game speed.
focus: Sharp pass to feet, then sprint past the defender
Return pass first-time, into the runner's path
Finish the move — composed strike on the proper goal

elem.title: 7v7 Big-Field Scrimmage
elem.desc: 7v7 with keepers. Coach challenge: goals from a give-and-go count double, and keepers are encouraged to start attacks with a thrown or driven ball — aerial first touches straight from Lesson 1.
focus: Look for the 1-2 around every defender

weeks.label: Week 7 — Full-Field Game Day & Season Finale

prek.title: Kids' Choice Warm-Up
prek.desc: The kids vote: Red Light Green Light, Sharks & Minnows, or Body Part Dribble. Their favorite from the summer, one last time. Big energy, big smiles, one more round of touches.
focus: Lots of touches on the ball
Bring the energy for game day

prek.title: Summer Skills Showcase
prek.desc: Every player shows off one skill from the summer — bells, a superhero move, bounce juggling, a big shot. Coach announces each kid like a stadium announcer. Confidence is the last lesson of the season.
focus: Pick your best skill and own it
It's okay to show off today

prek.title: Penalty Shootout on the Big Goal
prek.space: 8 yd spot to a proper goal
prek.desc: Every player takes penalties on the full-size goal with the coach in net (dramatic dives included). Everyone scores at least once — the coach's hands are mysteriously slow today. Photos and celebrations encouraged.
focus: Pick your corner before you run up

prek.title: Season Finale — 5v5 Championship
prek.desc: The last game of the summer on the full field with the proper goals. Everything we built — first touch, passing, superhero moves, big shots — in one joyful game. Coach celebrates every kid by name before the final whistle.

elem.title: World Cup Knockout + Juggling PR
elem.desc: Two-part opener: beat your Week 6 juggling record, then 1v1 World Cup knockout — winner stays, tournament bracket to a champion. The summer's competitive edge, one last time.
focus: Use your go-to move in the duels

elem.title: Positions & Shape on the Full Field
elem.desc: Quick chalk talk: where the defenders, midfielders, and strikers live on a full field, and how the shape stretches and squeezes with the ball. Players walk the shape, then jog it while the coach moves the ball. Everyone tries a position they haven't played.
focus: Where do I go WITHOUT the ball?
Stretch wide with the ball, squeeze without it

elem.title: Crossbar Challenge & Team Penalties
elem.desc: Two finale classics on the big goal: crossbar challenge (hit the bar from 18 yards) and a team penalty shootout with full walk-ups and keepers. Technique from Week 4 under the best kind of pressure — teammates watching.
focus: Clean laces strike for the crossbar
Penalties: pick a side and commit
Handle the pressure — breathe, then strike

elem.title: Season Finale — Full-Field 7v7
elem.desc: The finale: 7v7 with keepers, real positions from Lesson 1, on the biggest field we've had all summer. Coach referees and celebrates the season's growth in every sequence — first touches, combos, take-ons, finishes. End-of-summer awards after the whistle.
focus: Play your position, apply the whole summer
Celebrate your teammates — you built this together
